The ongoing evolution of mobile commerce within European marketplaces is reflected in the latest data, highlighting significant
trends in market penetration and marketplace take rates. As consumers increasingly adopt mobile platforms for their shopping
needs, the data underscores a growing mobility in transaction dynamics. Notably, the average order value for Q2 2023 reached
CHF65M, showcasing a gradual increase from CHF60M in Q1 2023. This trend is paralleled by the mobile conversion rate,
which stands at 20% for Q2 2023, indicating a robust engagement of users on mobile platforms. Additionally, mobile market
share continues to gain traction, with a recorded 85% in Q1 2023, demonstrating the strong preference for mobile transactions.
The mobile return rate for Q3 2023 reflects these trends, further indicating the need for retailers to adapt their strategies
accordingly.
MarketplaceMetrics  Q4Results
TakeRate  GrowthRate
Profitability  P/ERatio  CurrentRatio  Debt/Equity
In terms of financial performance, marketplace take rates have also seen fluctuations, with the Q3 2023 rate at 15%, a slight
improvement from earlier quarters. As we look toward Q4 2023, projected metrics suggest a potential decrease in mobile return
rates to 12.5%, emphasizing the importance of continuous monitoring of these indicators to drive future strategies. Overall, the
data signals a shift towards a more mobile-centric e-commerce landscape, necessitating adaptive strategies from marketplace
operators.
